resposta:
solution:
def validArrangement(pairs):
    result = []
    for start, end in pairs:
        result.append([end, start])
    return result

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
The solution iterates over the given pairs and appends the tuple (end, start) to the result list. This ensures that the end value becomes the start value for the next iteration, maintaining the condition that end(i-1) == start(i). The result list will contain all possible valid arrangements of the pairs. The time complexity is O(n) because the algorithm iterates over the pairs once, and the space complexity is O(n) due to the result list storing n elements.
